questions.question_text: How many total hours of continuing education must a DC licensee complete each renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: DC requires 15 hours of CE every 2 years for license renewal: 12 mandatory hours (fair housing, ethics, legislative update, property management) plus 3 elective hours.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a mandatory CE course topic for DC real estate licensees?
questions.explanation: DC mandates four 3-hour courses: DC Fair Housing, DC Ethics, DC Legislative Update, and Property Management. These 12 mandatory hours plus 3 elective hours total 15 hours.

questions.question_text: A DC licensee completes 12 hours of mandatory CE but no elective hours before the renewal deadline. What is the consequence?
distractors: The license becomes inactive for 90 days
The license renews with a probation period
questions.explanation: In DC, failure to complete all 15 CE hours by the deadline results in license expiration, not just inactive status. The licensee must meet reinstatement requirements to practice again.

questions.question_text: How many hours of elective CE must a DC licensee complete per renewal period?
questions.explanation: Of the 15 total CE hours required, 12 are mandatory (fair housing, ethics, legislative update, property management at 3 hours each) and 3 are elective on Commission-approved topics.

questions.question_text: Which mandatory CE topic helps DC licensees stay current on recent regulatory changes?
questions.explanation: The 3-hour DC Legislative Update course covers recent changes to DC real estate law and regulations, keeping licensees informed about new requirements. All four mandatory topics serve different purposes.

questions.question_text: When is the renewal deadline for DC salesperson licenses?
questions.explanation: DC salesperson licenses renew on August 31 of odd-numbered years. Broker licenses renew on February 28 of odd-numbered years. The two license types have different renewal cycles.

questions.question_text: What is the total cost to renew a DC real estate license, including the Guaranty Fund fee?
questions.explanation: The renewal fee is $130 plus a $60 Guaranty and Education Fund fee, totaling $190. The Guaranty Fund fee is collected from every licensee to maintain the consumer protection fund.

questions.question_text: A DC broker's renewal deadline is February 28. A DC salesperson's renewal deadline falls on which date?
questions.explanation: Brokers renew February 28 and salespersons renew August 31, both in odd-numbered years. The staggered deadlines allow DLCP to process renewals efficiently.

questions.question_text: How often must DC real estate licensees renew their licenses?
questions.explanation: DC real estate licenses are renewed on a biennial (2-year) cycle. Each renewal requires completion of 15 hours of continuing education and payment of the renewal fee.

questions.question_text: A licensee changes broker affiliation in DC. What must they do?
distractors: Complete an additional 6 hours of CE
Wait 30 days before practicing under the new broker
questions.explanation: Changes in broker affiliation must be promptly reported to DLCP. The licensee does not need to retake exams or complete additional education for a simple broker change.

questions.question_text: A DC licensee misses their renewal deadline by 30 days. What option is available?
questions.correct_answer: Late renewal with a $50 penalty fee
distractors: Automatic 90-day grace period at no cost
Must reapply as a new applicant
questions.explanation: DC allows late renewal up to 60 days after the deadline with a $50 penalty fee. Beyond 60 days, the license lapses and the licensee must meet reinstatement requirements.

questions.question_text: How long after the renewal deadline can a DC licensee still renew late?
questions.explanation: Late renewals are permitted up to 60 days after the renewal deadline with a $50 penalty fee. After 60 days, the license lapses and reinstatement procedures apply.

questions.question_text: A DC salesperson lets their license lapse for 90 days past the renewal deadline. What must they do to practice again?
questions.correct_answer: Meet reinstatement requirements set by the Commission
distractors: Simply pay the renewal fee plus late penalty
Wait until the next renewal cycle
questions.explanation: After the 60-day late renewal window expires, the license lapses entirely. The licensee must meet reinstatement requirements, which may include additional CE, fees, and Commission approval.

questions.question_text: What is the penalty fee for late renewal of a DC real estate license?
questions.explanation: The late renewal penalty is $50, in addition to the regular renewal fee of $130 and the $60 Guaranty Fund fee. This applies only during the 60-day late renewal window.

questions.question_text: In DC, what happens when a licensee fails to complete continuing education by the renewal deadline?
questions.correct_answer: The license expires rather than becoming inactive
distractors: The license automatically converts to inactive
The licensee gets a 6-month probation period
The CE requirement is waived for one cycle
questions.explanation: Unlike many states that place licenses on inactive status, DC's license expires entirely if CE is not completed by the deadline. This is a stricter consequence than most jurisdictions.
